BIG LABOR DAY

pages.raw_text:
VII F6SS esNS't
to Bernard, south on Bernard to
First, west on First to Lincoln and
countermarch on First to Bernard,
the point of dismissal.


AT 1870 p. m. the speaking will
begin at Natatorium park. with w.
j. Coates as presiding officer. The
speakers will be H. A. Jarvis, pres
ident of the Central Labor council;
Mrs. p. P. Stalled, representing
the suffrage movement; r. B. Mar
tin. president of the Spokane Coun-
ty Good Government club, and
Charles Perry Taylor of Tacoma,
who will deliver the chief address
OF the day. z Z z z


AT the conclusion of the speak
ing a varied program of sports and
athletic contests of different kinds
will be given and not the least
item of interest being a baby show
for the wives of union men. At
night a grand Labor day ball will
be given in the dancing pavilion at
the park.
